name: Video Stream Out TOP
description: Note: This TOP uses the Nvidia Hardware Encoder to create the stream and therefore requires an Nvidia GPU and Windows to operate.
summary: Note: This TOP uses the Nvidia Hardware Encoder to create the stream and therefore requires an Nvidia GPU and Windows to operate.

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Controls if the server is active or not.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description:
mode -  - Selects if the mode works as an RTSP server, sends RTMP to a receiever such as a distribution service like YouTube or Twitch, or sends to an SRT destination.

 rtspserver - Use the RTSP and RTP protocol. More information here. rtmpsender - Use the RTMP protocol. More information here. srt - Use the SRT protocol. More information here. webrtc - Use a WebRTC peer. More info: WebRTC, WebRTC DAT.

parameters.name: RTSP Server
parameters.label: RTSP Server
parameters.description: rtspserver - Use the RTSP and RTP protocol. More information here. rtmpsender - Use the RTMP protocol. More information here. srt - Use the SRT protocol. More information here. webrtc - Use a WebRTC peer. More info: WebRTC, WebRTC DAT.

parameters.name: Network Port
parameters.label: Network Port
parameters.description: port - The port the server should listen on. Multiple Video Stream Out TOPs can use the same port as long as each has a unique Stream Name.

parameters.name: Stream Name
parameters.label: Stream Name
parameters.description: streamname - The name of the stream for this node. This name is what comes after the / in the URL after the ipaddress:port combination.

parameters.name: Multi-Cast
parameters.label: Multi-Cast
parameters.description: multicast - Controls if RTSP server sends its video out using unicast or multicast UDP packets.

parameters.name: RTMP Destination URL
parameters.label: RTMP Destination URL
parameters.description: url - The URL to sent the RTMP stream to. This should be in the format of {service url}/{stream key}. For example for twitch the URL would be something like rtmp://live-yto.twitch.tv/app/live_1234567_sduhy3xJ1KJ34Eg6CjksdJLubFS7gtUY. You may need to search to find the correct URL depending on your location and the service you are using.

parameters.name: Destination URL
parameters.label: Destination URL
parameters.description: url - The URL to sent the RTMP stream to. This should be in the format of {service url}/{stream key}. For example for twitch the URL would be something like rtmp://live-yto.twitch.tv/app/live_1234567_sduhy3xJ1KJ34Eg6CjksdJLubFS7gtUY. You may need to search to find the correct URL depending on your location and the service you are using.

parameters.name: Force IDR
parameters.label: Force IDR
parameters.description: forceidr - For debugging, this will force the server to create a new video keyframe to send to all the clients. If clients aren't getting proper image this can be used to attempt to fix it. If you need to use this parameter please report the case to support@derivative.ca.

parameters.name: FPS
parameters.label: FPS
parameters.description: fps - The  to send video at.

parameters.name: Video Codec
parameters.label: Video Codec
parameters.description:
videocodec -  - Select which codec to use for encoding the stream.

 h264nvgpu - h265nvgpu -

parameters.name: H.264 (NVIDIA GPU)
parameters.label: H.264 (NVIDIA GPU)
parameters.description: h264nvgpu - h265nvgpu -

parameters.name: Profile
parameters.label: Profile
parameters.description:
profile -  - The H.264 profile to use to encode the frames. Some decoders can only support H.264 encoder at certain profiles.

 baseline - main - high -

parameters.name: Baseline
parameters.label: Baseline
parameters.description: baseline - main - high -

parameters.name: Quality
parameters.label: Quality
parameters.description:
quality -  - The quality level of the encoding.

 lowlatencylow - lowlatencymedium - lowlatencyhigh - highlatencylow - highlatencyhigh -

parameters.name: Low-Latency Low
parameters.label: Low-Latency Low
parameters.description: lowlatencylow - lowlatencymedium - lowlatencyhigh - highlatencylow - highlatencyhigh -

parameters.name: Keyframe Interval
parameters.label: Keyframe Interval
parameters.description: keyframeinterval - Set the keyframe interval for the H.264 encoder.

parameters.name: Max B-Frames
parameters.label: Max B-Frames
parameters.description: maxbframes - The maximum number of bi-directional frames that can occur between keyframes. More will increase latency but reduce bandwidth.

parameters.name: Intra-Refresh Period
parameters.label: Intra-Refresh Period
parameters.description: intrarefreshperiod - Intra-refresh is a gradual keyframe that is applied across the image to clean up streaming artifacts over multiple frames, instead of one large keyframe. This controls the number of frames that elapse between each intra-refresh occuring.

parameters.name: Intra-Refresh Length
parameters.label: Intra-Refresh Length
parameters.description: intrarefreshlength - The number of frames the intra-refresh will be spread out across.

parameters.name: Bitrate Mode
parameters.label: Bitrate Mode
parameters.description:
bitratemode -  - Chooses between constant (CBR) and variable (VBR) bit rate modes. Mode streaming services prefer a constant bit rate mode.

 constant - variable - constanthq - variablehq -

parameters.name: Constant (CBR)
parameters.label: Constant (CBR)
parameters.description: constant - variable - constanthq - variablehq -

parameters.name: Average Bitrate (Mb/s)
parameters.label: Average Bitrate (Mb/s)
parameters.description: avgbitrate - The target bitrate for the encoding. This is specified in Mb/s (megabits/second).

parameters.name: Per-Frame Metadata
parameters.label: Per-Frame Metadata
parameters.description: perframemetadata - Send metadata from this OP with each frame of the video stream. This data can be received from the Video Stream In TOP using an Info CHOP and Info DAT.

parameters.name: Max Bitrate (Mb/s)
parameters.label: Max Bitrate (Mb/s)
parameters.description: maxbitrate - The maximum bitrate for the encoding. This is specified in Mb/s (megabits/second).

parameters.name: Num H264 Slices per Frame
parameters.label: Num H264 Slices per Frame
parameters.description: numslices - This controls how many pieces (slices) each H.264 frame is separated into. Some decoders are able to decode multiple slices simultaneously so setting this to a value above 1 allows those decoders to run more efficiently.

parameters.name: Audio CHOP
parameters.label: Audio CHOP
parameters.description: audiochop - A timesliced audio source to send along with the video. For RTSP, Audio will be resampled to 44100Hz before being encoded into MP3. For RTMP the sample rate must already be 44100. For WebRTC the sample rate must be 48000.

parameters.name: Audio Bit Rate
parameters.label: Audio Bit Rate
parameters.description:
audiobitrate -  - Set the bit rate used for encoding audio.

 b96 - b128 - b192 - b256 - b320 -

parameters.name: Include Silent Audio Stream
parameters.label: Include Silent Audio Stream
parameters.description:
includesilentaudio -  - Some broadcasting services require an audio stream to be included. This will include a silent audio stream along with the video in the event there isn't actual audio being streamed video the  parameter.

 automatic - on - off -

parameters.name: Automatic
parameters.label: Automatic
parameters.description: automatic - on - off -

parameters.name: Per-Frame Metadata CHOP/DAT
parameters.label: Per-Frame Metadata CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: perframemetadata - Send metadata from this OP with each frame of the video stream. This data can be recevied from the Video Stream In TOP using an Info CHOP and Info DAT.

parameters.name: WebRTC
parameters.label: WebRTC
parameters.description: webrtc - Set the WebRTC DAT (ie. peer) to send the video stream over. Setting this will automatically populate the WebRTC Connection parameter menu with available connections.

parameters.name: WebRTC Connection
parameters.label: WebRTC Connection
parameters.description: webrtcconnection - Select the WebRTC peer-to-peer connection. Selecting this will automatically population the WebRTC Track parameter menu with available video output tracks.

parameters.name: WebRTC Video Track
parameters.label: WebRTC Video Track
parameters.description: webrtcvideotrack - Select the video output track that's a part of the WebRTC peer-to-peer connection.

parameters.name: WebRTC Audio Track
parameters.label: WebRTC Audio Track
parameters.description: webrtcaudiotrack - Optionally select the audio output track that's a part of the WebRTC peer-to-peer connection, to be sent along with the video.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. Making this larger than 1 is essentially the same as taking the output from each pass, and passing it into the first input of the node and repeating the process. Other inputs and parameters remain the same for each pass.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
